summary: High‑energy Vegas show with blazing Eyes and killer China>Rider jam
blurb: The set bursts open with a rapid "Eyes Of The World" and rolls into a classic "China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider" sequence. Brent delivers an all‑time best solo on "It's All Over Now" while Phil’s bass stays solidly "on" throughout. Recording is clean enough to enjoy the highs, though vocal flubs and a brief set‑list tension linger.
review: April 6, 1984 at the Aladdin Theater captured one of those rare Vegas nights where the Dead seemed to be running on pure adrenaline. The first set builds momentum with fast‑paced jams – "Eyes Of The World" is blistering, and even the sloppy finish to "The Music Never Stopped" shows a playful tension between Jerry and Bob that adds a human edge. The second set is where the night truly shines: a seamless "China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider" leads into the soaring "Lost Sailor → Saint Of Circumstance" flow, capped by an unforgettable keyboard solo on "It's All Over Now" that many fans call Brent’s best ever. Though the soundboard capture has its share of crackle and a few vocal missteps, the overall energy, tight rhythm section, and standout improvisations make this show a must‑listen for anyone craving a high‑octane Dead experience.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: One recording is clearly positive, highlighting "U.S. Blues" and overall excitement; the other is mixed, praising fast jams and Brent's solo while noting vocal flubs and a set‑list argument. The balance leans modestly positive, warranting a moderate sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with generally clean instrument separation and full set coverage, despite minor crackle and vocal glitches.

key_highlights: Blazing "Eyes Of The World" that launches the second set.
Classic "China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider" jam with Brent’s chimey keyboards.
All‑time best Brent solo on "It's All Over Now" (keyboard spotlight).
High energy flow despite occasional vocal flubs and a brief band tension.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers a thunderous, fast‑paced lead on "Eyes Of The World" and drives the set with high voltage guitar, though early vocal flubs and a brief argument with Bob over song order add tension.
band_performance.Phil: Stays rock solid throughout; multiple reviewers note his bass is "on" and provides a firm foundation for the band’s high‑energy excursions.
band_performance.Bob: Adds fiery slide fills on "Black Peter" and contributes strong rhythm, but slips on lyrics in "The Music Never Stopped" and briefly clashes with Jerry over set transitions.
band_performance.Brent: Offers an all‑time best keyboard solo on "It's All Over Now" and his chimey voicings lift the "China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider" sequence to new heights.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums rise in volume during improvisations, adding height to jams; long tuning pauses before the "Jerry‑less" jam are noted but don’t detract from overall flow.
band_performance.Billy: Works with Mickey to create a soaring drum presence; his rhythmic drive supports the rapid set pace and helps lift the extended second‑set jams.
